operation: First, a rectangular base block with dimensions 66 mm (X-axis) × 10 mm (Y-axis) × 70 mm (Z-axis) is created on the front workplane. Next, a new workplane is established on the maximum Y face of the block. The workplane origin is offset 2 mm along the Z-axis, then a 47.1 mm × 47 mm construction rectangle is drawn. Counterbore holes with a 5 mm through diameter, 7.5 mm counterbore diameter, and 4 mm counterbore depth are drilled at each of the four vertices of the construction rectangle. Finally, a larger central counterbore hole with a 28.2 mm through diameter, 40 mm counterbore diameter, and 2 mm counterbore depth is drilled at the center point of the same construction rectangle, aligned with the offset origin.
geometry: This model is a uniform-thickness rectangular prism plate. Five through counterbore holes are recessed into one of its larger flat faces: four identically sized small counterbore holes are arranged at the four corners of an invisible rectangular layout, while a single larger counterbore hole sits at the exact center of that same rectangular layout. All holes pass fully through the thickness of the plate.
function: This is a mechanical mounting plate intended for assembly use. The four smaller corner holes support fastener attachment to secure the plate in position, while the large central hole accommodates pass-through components such as shafts, connectors, or protruding assembly parts.
